title: See Syracuse’s 25 most dangerous intersections (and how the city plans to make them safer)
summary: Mayor Walsh seeks Common Council approval of Syracuse's Vision Zero Action Plan, targeting 25 intersections and 25 miles of roadway where 50% of serious injury/fatal crashes occurred (2019-2023). The plan proposes infrastructure improvements (crosswalk visibility, lane reductions, lighting, speed cameras) to achieve zero fatalities by 2055 with 35% reduction by 2035. Public Works Committee meets July 28 for vote.

political_signal.description: Mayor-led Vision Zero initiative demonstrates executive commitment to public safety and data-driven infrastructure investment. Addresses equity by concentrating resources on 1% of intersections responsible for 50% of serious injuries/fatalities.

processing_notes: Article references Vision Zero Action Plan as primary policy document—recommend CIO review for data governance, GIS mapping, and analytics infrastructure requirements
Mentions 2+ years of data analysis and community feedback—potential IT systems integration for ongoing crash data monitoring and outcome measurement
Timeline: Public Works Committee 7/28; vote expected same date—time-sensitive for implementation planning
No budget figures provided; recommend cross-reference with capital planning documents for fiscal impact
References syr.gov/vision-zero resource—verify digital infrastructure and public dashboard accessibility
